evidence: Sketch endpoints (/api/sketch/*, /api/relations) omit 'status: ok' from the response envelope; all exploration and semantic-field endpoints include it (ExplorationHandlers.java:~93, ~152, ~190, ~220; SketchHandlers.java:handleFullSketchForType, handleRelationQueryInternal — no status key added)

Count-field naming is inconsistent across endpoints: explore responses use suffixed '*_count' keys (seed_collocates_count, discovered_nouns_count, core_collocates_count); comparison endpoint uses 'total_adjectives', 'total_edges'; concordance uses 'total' + 'limit_requested'; BCQL uses 'total' + 'limit'; examples uses 'count'

SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern wraps numeric options in ExploreOptions (topCollocates, nounsPerCollocate, minLogDice, minShared, verbose), while SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreMultiSeed takes the same conceptual options as individual primitives (minLogDice, topCollocates, minShared) — non-parallel public signatures for closely related operations

The per-relation object shape diverges between full-sketch and single-relation responses: full SURFACE sketch includes 'cql' and 'collocate_pos_group'; single-relation response adds 'id' and 'total_matches' but drops 'cql' and 'collocate_pos_group' (SketchHandlers.java lines ~140-150 vs ~209-213)

strengths: HTTP method enforcement is applied uniformly: registerGetHandler and registerPostHandler wrap every route with OPTIONS/method-check logic, so no endpoint accidentally accepts the wrong verb
Error propagation is uniform: HttpApiUtils.sendError and requireParam are used consistently across all handlers, giving callers a predictable JSON error shape on every failure path

The QueryExecutor interface distinguishes closely related operations (findCollocations vs executeSurfacePattern vs executeBcqlQuery) with clear per-method Javadoc contracts, so implementors know exactly what each method must do

Single-seed and multi-seed explore responses share four standard sections (seed_collocates, discovered_nouns, core_collocates, edges) populated by the shared buildExploreResponseBody helper, so the two endpoints are structurally parallel
api_surface_coherence.issue_character: The issues are convention-drift across related areas rather than structural breakage: the same conceptual slots (status envelope, result counts, numeric option grouping) are named or shaped differently depending on which handler or domain the endpoint belongs to.
api_surface_coherence.score_rationale: The API layer is well-structured with consistent error handling and HTTP plumbing, placing it above the 60-band where APIs are 'hard to predict'. However, a developer writing a general client would encounter recurring surprises: sketch endpoints lack the status envelope that all other endpoints include, count fields are named differently across endpoints, and the SemanticFieldExplorer public API switches between an options-object convention and individual primitives for the same conceptual parameters. These recurring irregularities place the codebase solidly in the 70-band ('a developer would encounter enough inconsistency that they can't fully trust patterns they've seen elsewhere'), with the score pulled down slightly toward 68 by the per-relation response shape divergence that makes client-side sketch parsing non-uniform.

issues.summary: Sketch and relations endpoints omit 'status: ok' present in all other endpoint responses
evidence: handleFullSketchForType builds the response map with 'lemma' and 'relations' keys but no 'status' key
handleRelationsForType wraps response in Collections.singletonMap("relations", ...) — no status
All ExplorationHandlers methods explicitly set response.put("status", "ok") before returning
issues.suggestion: Add response.put("status", "ok") at the top of handleFullSketchForType, handleRelationQueryInternal, and handleRelationsForType in SketchHandlers.java so that every endpoint returns a uniform top-level status field.

issues.summary: Result-count fields use four different naming conventions across endpoints
evidence: Explore response uses suffixed '*_count' keys: seed_collocates_count, discovered_nouns_count, core_collocates_count (buildExploreResponseBody)
Concordance endpoint uses 'total' for result count plus 'limit_requested' for the cap (handleConcordanceExamples)
BCQL endpoint uses 'total' for result count plus 'limit' for the cap (handleBcqlQueryPost)
Examples endpoint uses 'count' for result count (handleSemanticFieldExamples)
issues.suggestion: Pick one convention for result-count fields (e.g., 'total' for top-level counts, 'count' as a per-list suffix) and apply it uniformly. For the limit/cap field, use a single name ('limit') across concordance and BCQL endpoints. Update handleSemanticFieldComparison, handleConcordanceExamples, handleBcqlQueryPost, handleSemanticFieldExamples, and buildExploreResponseBody together.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreMultiSeed takes raw primitives where the sibling exploreByPattern takes ExploreOptions
evidence: exploreByPattern(seed, RelationConfig, ExploreOptions) — options grouped as ExploreOptions record
exploreMultiSeed(Set<String> seeds, RelationConfig, double minLogDice, int topCollocates, int minShared) — same three numeric options passed individually
ExploreOptions already exists and holds exactly minLogDice, topCollocates (as topCollocates), and minShared — it is not reused in exploreMultiSeed
issues.suggestion: Change exploreMultiSeed to accept ExploreOptions instead of the three individual numeric parameters. The 'nounsPerCollocate' field in ExploreOptions is not applicable to multi-seed and can be documented as ignored, or a new slim record can be introduced; either is better than the current asymmetry.

issues.summary: Per-relation object shape differs between full-sketch and single-relation SURFACE responses
evidence: handleFullSketchForType (SURFACE branch, lines ~147-148) includes 'cql' and 'collocate_pos_group' in the per-relation object but omits 'total_matches'
handleRelationQueryInternal (lines ~209-213) includes 'id' and 'total_matches' but omits 'cql' and 'collocate_pos_group'
A client parsing /api/sketch/{lemma} cannot reuse the same per-relation parsing logic for /api/sketch/{lemma}/{relation}
issues.suggestion: Unify the per-relation object by extracting a formatRelationData(RelationConfig rel, List<WordSketchResult> results, boolean isDep) helper and using it in both code paths. Include 'id', 'name', 'total_matches', 'collocations', and — for SURFACE — also 'cql' and 'collocate_pos_group' in both responses.

root_causes: Incremental endpoint addition without a shared response-builder contract: each handler assembles its own response map, so envelope conventions (status field, count naming) drift across handlers
ExploreOptions was introduced after exploreMultiSeed was written (or vice versa) without retrofitting the sibling method
